column1: CFS QUEUE CRISIS MATRIX — VESSEL CUT-OFF MISS ? ALTERNATIVE OPTIONS & TIME SAVINGS
Goal: D42 ? D40 ? D38 (or better) | Decision Framework: Hours Saved | Cost Impact | Buyer Approval Required
SCENARIO: Long CFS queue ? Vessel cut-off missed OR Early factory dispatch available ? Exploit earlier vessel
CFS queue > 48 hrs — Vessel cut-off MISSED
column13: Activate Sea-Air immediately. Notify buyer T+2h. Get written approval. Rebook carrier.
Check FV space immediately. Submit revised booking. Update buyer ETA proactively.

column1: Nominated carrier has NO space (CFS to FV)
column13: Immediate: check Tailwind/Hapag for same-week space. If none, escalate to Sea-Air.

column10: Air from T/S hub for critical POs
column13: Assess at T/S: if rollover >5d, air remaining leg. Split shipment if partial urgent.

column1: Port congestion at POL (>72 hrs delay)
column13: Reroute truck to INMAA or INBOM. Alert buyer. Inland cost increases but sea time saved.

column1: Early cargo — Factory ready D-5 ahead of plan
column13: Best case: book earlier FV immediately. Update PO ETA. Buyer gets early delivery = goodwill.

column10: Switch to Seaway BL / Express Surrender
column13: Immediately apply for Express BL or seaway bill. Confirm with buyer on BL type acceptance.

column1: QC FAIL at factory — Reinspection needed +D7
column3: Hold cargo; catch next vessel week+1
column7: Full air for critical season cargo
column10: Air for critical PO; sea for non-critical
column13: Bifurcate: critical POs ? air. Non-critical ? wait for next vessel. Inform buyer in <4 hrs.
